threads.subject: Solis Clayco Billing
threads.summary: Active billing dispute. Ryan's June 26 email questioned Estopinal's invoice schedule, asking why three invoices had been submitted (down payment + April + May) and flagging need to discuss extended precon billing beyond July. Estopinal counter-responded attaching signed agreement and claiming the agreement explicitly shows 4 equal monthly payments — not prepayments. This is a contract interpretation dispute between Ryan/Clayco and Pacific Fusion on billing structure. Tinney said 'Thank you sir! Have a great weekend' (appears to support Estopinal's position). Dispute is unresolved.
key_facts: Ryan's position (June 26 16:58): Questioned 3-invoice structure (down payment + April + May) and raised extended precon billing beyond July
Estopinal's counter (June 26): Signed agreement = 4 equal monthly payments (not prepayments), attached signed copy
Tinney response: 'Thank you sir! Have a great weekend' (ambiguous — may be supporting Estopinal)
has_attachment: true (signed agreement on billing structure)
Ryan also cc'd Nick Rivera (PF) on his initial response
has_contract_language: true
Solis Project Schedule Development meeting is today at 12:30PM MST (Tinney + Ryan + Whitmer)

action_items.text: Review signed agreement Estopinal attached — confirm actual billing terms before Solis Schedule meeting at 12:30PM today. Determine Ryan's position on extended precon billing beyond July
Align with Tinney on billing dispute before Solis Schedule meeting — understand his position (appears he sided with Estopinal)

commitments.text: Raised extended precon billing beyond July — needs resolution discussion

threads.subject: Project Solis - Foundation Selection / Building Reduction
threads.summary: WGI (Ken) answered Bellum's structural question: rigid inclusions do NOT have reinforcing. Low strength grout element (~750 psi, ~5ft edge-to-edge). This means the mat foundation does NOT need to span between point supports — the previous structural risk (from the June 26 last-raw) is resolved. Bellum acknowledged with 'Thank you.' Engineering coordination continues but the main structural uncertainty is cleared.
key_facts: Ken (WGI): No reinforcing in rigid inclusions — low strength grout element
WGI specs confirmed: ~750 psi, ~5ft edge-to-edge
Bellum (Stantec): acknowledged — 'Thank you. RB'
RESOLVED: Mat foundation does NOT need to span rigid inclusions as point supports
Structural cost risk from June 26 (TT mat design complexity) is mitigated

action_items.text: Confirm TT (Koenigs/Fang/Leung) received WGI's answer and updated their mat design assumptions — ensure no cost change required

risk_signals: TT still needs to confirm their mat design reflects WGI's answer (no rebar, low-strength grout = no point support spanning needed)
Building reduction (grid 10-12) scope change still needs to be tracked for contract impact
